passed.name: Claude 4 Extended
passed.external_validation_summary: Arena benchmark shows competitive ranking with GPT-4. HuggingFace ranked #2 trending models this week.
passed.real_change_notes: Context window expansion is measurable; 30% reasoning improvement needs independent validation
passed.selection_impact_notes: Changes model selection for long-document workflows (financial analysis, legal review, research synthesis)
passed.price_speed_cost_notes: $3/MTok input (12.5% price increase vs 4.0); speed comparable to GPT-4; cost per context unit decreased
passed.app_api_workflow_notes: Direct API upgrade; no workflow change; chat interface unchanged; 48-hour enablement for existing users
new_use_cases: Multi-document analysis (200K context enables 40+ research papers in single request)
Code repository comprehension (full codebase in one interaction)
Long-form creative writing with extended consistency
passed.business_model: Subscription pricing maintained; context-based billing encourages longer interactions
passed.ecosystem_echo: Early adoption signals from research teams; Hugging Face trending spike indicates ecosystem attention
passed.durability_notes: Context window expansion is durable; expected to remain relevant for 2+ months as baseline
passed.risk_caveat: Quality degradation risk at extreme context lengths; real-world latency impact not yet public
passed.one_line_judgment: Substantial but incremental upgrade; context expansion matters for specific workflows, pricing increase marginal
passed.relevance_to_us: Enables new creative workflows (long-form synthesis); moderate impact on product roadmap decisions

passed.name: Gemini 2 Pro
official_claims: Native video input (up to 1 hour per request)
passed.external_validation_summary: TechCrunch early hands-on noted video quality improvements; latency claim not independently verified
passed.real_change_notes: Video understanding is new capability; speed improvement measurable but not externally verified
passed.selection_impact_notes: Video input capability changes model selection for multimodal workflows (video analysis, transcription); latency improvement incremental
passed.price_speed_cost_notes: No price change; latency improvement reduces cost-per-token effectively; speed claimed but unverified
passed.app_api_workflow_notes: API and Gemini Advanced UI updated simultaneously; video upload workflow added
new_use_cases: Video content analysis (YouTube videos, recorded meetings, training materials)
Long-form video transcription with context understanding
Technical video debugging (screen recordings, error logs)
passed.business_model: Subscription maintained ($20/month); video inputs encourage higher engagement within tier
passed.ecosystem_echo: Google ecosystem integration signals (YouTube, Workspace); limited third-party developer signals yet
passed.durability_notes: Video capability expected to remain relevant for 6+ months; latency improvements likely temporary as competitors catch up
passed.risk_caveat: Video understanding quality highly dependent on content type; hallucination risk on complex scenes not disclosed
passed.one_line_judgment: Multimodal expansion significant; video capability novel but quality validation pending
passed.relevance_to_us: Video workflows are emerging in creative tools; direct relevance to future product expansion

passed.name: Llama 3.2 Open
official_claims: Competitive with proprietary models at similar scale
passed.external_validation_summary: HuggingFace trending #1. Community reproduction on Reddit r/LocalLLaMA reports positive instruction-following; benchmarks not yet published by independent sources.
passed.real_change_notes: Instruction-following improvement real but magnitude unclear; no published benchmarks yet
passed.selection_impact_notes: Open-weight release critical for edge deployment; selection impact high for cost-conscious teams
passed.price_speed_cost_notes: Free (open-weight); inference cost determined by host hardware (typically 40-60% cheaper than proprietary alternatives)
passed.app_api_workflow_notes: Run locally on GPU or cloud instances; no official API; community integrations emerging (LM Studio, Ollama)
new_use_cases: Fine-tuned model creation (Apache 2.0 allows commercial derivative works)
Edge deployment (on-device AI for privacy-sensitive workflows)
Self-hosted AI products (startups building moats via custom Llama variants)
passed.business_model: Open-source; enables commercial derivatives and self-hosted deployments
passed.ecosystem_echo: Strong community adoption signals (HF #1 trending); multiple project forks visible on GitHub within 48 hours
passed.durability_notes: Open-weight foundation creates durable ecosystem; expected to support derivative research for 12+ months
passed.risk_caveat: Community-only support; no vendor guarantee; quality depends on deployment context
passed.one_line_judgment: Open-weight release with solid instruction-following; strongest ecosystem echo among this week's releases
passed.relevance_to_us: Direct relevance for edge product strategies and fine-tuning workflows

passed.name: GPT-4 Turbo v2
passed.external_validation_summary: No independent third-party validation yet; rollout completed without public incidents
passed.real_change_notes: Function calling improvement specific and measurable; magnitude unclear without benchmark data
passed.selection_impact_notes: Function calling reliability matters for agent workflows; improvement moderate impact
passed.price_speed_cost_notes: Pricing unchanged; no stated latency improvement; cost-per-task may improve if function calling success rate is higher
passed.app_api_workflow_notes: API only; no chat interface change; requires explicit function schema definition; backward compatible
new_use_cases: Reliable multi-step task automation (invoicing, report generation)
Agent workflows with deterministic branching (execution no longer fails on malformed calls)
passed.business_model: Consumption-based pricing; higher reliability may increase API usage
passed.ecosystem_echo: Minimal visible ecosystem reaction; no third-party announcements yet
passed.durability_notes: Function calling is stable capability; improvements expected to remain relevant for 3+ months
passed.risk_caveat: Rollout in progress; corner cases may emerge; 98% accuracy claim not independently audited
passed.one_line_judgment: Incremental reliability improvement for agent workflows; low ecosystem echo
passed.relevance_to_us: Moderate relevance for tool-calling product features

passed.name: Cursor Pro
passed.core_positioning: AI-first code editor with real-time collaboration for distributed teams
passed.problem_it_solves: Pair programming and code review friction in distributed development; context-switching to chat tools
passed.user_visibility: Immediate: users can activate in IDE settings; free tier available
passed.access_barrier_change: Free tier unlocked for up to 2 collaborators (previously enterprise-only feature)
passed.workflow_change: Enables async collaborative coding without context switching; single-pane-of-glass for code + collaboration
passed.distribution_change: Native IDE feature (no separate tool); automatic plugin distribution via marketplace
passed.new_product_form: Native IDE plugin with presence-aware collaboration layer
passed.interaction_pattern: Presence indicators + shared cursor + in-line code suggestions; drag-drop file sharing
user_scenarios: Pair programming across time zones (async code review with inline suggestions)
Code review without screen sharing (real-time diff with commentary)
Mentorship without scheduling (mentor joins, leaves comments, student iterates)
passed.business_model: Freemium: free for 2 collaborators; $30/month for unlimited + priority support
passed.risk_caveat: Sync latency on large diffs (100KB+); git conflict handling still manual
passed.market_signal_strength: Product Hunt #1 ranking (24h); 500+ upvotes; 50+ comment thread
passed.relevance_to_us: Direct relevance to distributed team workflows and collaborative creation
passed.one_line_judgment: Solves real collaboration friction; freemium model smart; requires user testing before full assessment

passed.name: Midjourney Web Studio
passed.launch_signal: Official product announcement + ProductHunt simultaneous launch
passed.core_positioning: First-class web interface for image generation (Discord no longer required as primary interaction model)
passed.problem_it_solves: Discord is not a design tool; creative workflows suffer; requires context switching between chat and image iteration
passed.user_visibility: Available to all paid subscribers (May 2, 2026); free tier delayed to Q2 2026
passed.access_barrier_change: Removes Discord dependency barrier; enables web/mobile access; lowers onboarding friction
passed.workflow_change: Enables single-app workflows for creators (prompt → iterate → export); real-time previews; design-app-native UX
passed.distribution_change: Web-first instead of Discord-first; mobile-accessible; no app download required
passed.new_product_form: Native web SPA with real-time canvas editing and batch processing
passed.interaction_pattern: Drag-drop prompts, in-browser upscaling, inpainting, style transfer; image gallery management
user_scenarios: Rapid iteration on design concepts (10 variations in < 2 minutes vs Discord threading)
Mobile-based image generation (iPad design app experience)
Client handoff without Discord accounts (professional workflow integration)
passed.business_model: Maintains subscription pricing ($10–$120/month) on web; tier structure unchanged
passed.risk_caveat: Discord integration still exists; may fragment user base; web app not yet feature-parity with planned roadmap
passed.market_signal_strength: ProductHunt launch day #1 position; 1000+ upvotes; trending on Twitter
passed.relevance_to_us: Direct relevance to creative workflows and tool design patterns
passed.one_line_judgment: Removes friction point for image generation; web-first positioning aligns with tool maturation trend

passed.name: Claude Code Sync
passed.launch_signal: Official Anthropic product announcement; HN discussion
passed.core_positioning: Seamless bridge between AI assistant and local dev environment; bi-directional file sync
passed.problem_it_solves: Manual copy-paste between Claude and IDE is friction; context fragmentation across tools
passed.user_visibility: Available now for Claude Teams subscribers (launch day)
passed.access_barrier_change: Teams-only feature (requires subscription escalation); not available to free tier
passed.workflow_change: Enables AI-assisted coding without leaving IDE; reduces context fragmentation; natural iterative loop
passed.distribution_change: IDE plugin ecosystem (VSCode Marketplace + JetBrains); automatic download via package manager
passed.new_product_form: Bidirectional sync protocol + IDE plugin; git-aware (respects .gitignore)
passed.interaction_pattern: Toggle sync in IDE → project state reflects Claude Projects → changes push back automatically
user_scenarios: AI-assisted refactoring with live feedback (change signature, sync, get suggestions)
Pair programming (human-AI) within IDE context (no alt-tab required)
Testing Claude suggestions without leaving IDE (branch, sync, run tests, commit)
passed.business_model: Claude Teams feature ($30/month included); drives upsell from Pro to Teams tier
passed.risk_caveat: Beta: occasional sync lag (2-5s); limited to Claude (no multi-model support); large files (>10MB) unsupported
passed.market_signal_strength: HN frontpage discussion; 200+ points; developer sentiment positive
passed.relevance_to_us: Direct relevance to coding workflows and AI-native development patterns
passed.one_line_judgment: Solves real IDE friction; Teams-only limitation may limit short-term adoption

passed.name: PixelGenius
passed.core_positioning: Desktop-first batch photo enhancement powered by open-source Flux model; local processing (no cloud upload)
passed.problem_it_solves: Batch photo enhancement usually requires cloud upload + subscription; privacy concerns
passed.access_barrier_change: One-time purchase ($49) vs monthly subscriptions; eliminates recurring cost + cloud privacy concerns
passed.workflow_change: Batch process photos offline; integrates with photo library apps; export directly to camera roll
passed.distribution_change: Desktop app (Electron) → planned mobile Q3 2026 → potential API for professional tools
passed.new_product_form: Electron app with bundled local model inference; no cloud dependency
passed.interaction_pattern: Drag folder → select enhancement level → export; batch processing in background
user_scenarios: Batch enhance photo library before archival (100+ photos in one action)
Real estate agent bulk process listings (consistency + speed)
Content creator prep stock photography (color grading, noise reduction)
passed.business_model: $49 one-time purchase (perpetual license); optional cloud backup add-on ($99/year)
passed.risk_caveat: Desktop-only initially; requires GPU (slower on CPU); Flux license compliance unclear for commercial use
passed.market_signal_strength: ProductHunt #2 trending; 300+ upvotes; Reddit photography thread (+50 karma)
passed.relevance_to_us: Moderate relevance to creative workflows; local-first model pattern interesting
passed.one_line_judgment: Solves real pain for photographers; startup team unknown; profitability model promising
